{
  "filePath": "/workspace/django/utils/deprecation.py",
  "newString": "import functools\nimport inspect\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport warnings\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom inspect import iscoroutinefunction\n\ntry:\n    from inspect import markcoroutinefunction\nexcept ImportError:\n    # markcoroutinefunction was added in Python 3.13\n    # For older versions, we'll use a no-op replacement\n    def markcoroutinefunction(func):\n        return func\n\nfrom asgiref.sync import sync_to_async\n\nimport django",
  "oldString": "import functools\nimport inspect\nimport os\nimport warnings\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom inspect import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\n\nfrom asgiref.sync import sync_to_async\n\nimport django",
  "originalFile": "import functools\nimport inspect\nimport os\nimport warnings\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom inspect import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\n\nfrom asgiref.sync import sync_to_async\n\nimport django\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef django_file_prefixes():\n    try:\n        file = django.__file__\n    except AttributeError:\n        return ()\n    return (os.path.dirname(file),)\n\n\nclass RemovedInNextVersionWarning(DeprecationWarning):\n    pass\n\n\nclass RemovedInDjango70Warning(PendingDeprecationWarning):\n    pass\n\n\nRemovedAfterNextVersionWarning = RemovedInDjango70Warning\n\n\nclass warn_about_renamed_method:\n    def __init__(\n        self, class_name, old_method_name, new_method_name, deprecation_warning\n    ):\n        self.class_name = class_name\n        self.old_method_name = old_method_name\n        self.new_method_name = new_method_name\n        self.deprecation_warning = deprecation_warning\n\n    def __call__(self, f):\n        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n            warnings.warn(\n                \"`%s.%s` is deprecated, use `%s` instead.\"\n                % (self.class_name, self.old_method_name, self.new_method_name),\n                self.deprecation_warning,\n                2,\n            )\n            return f(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        return wrapper\n\n\nclass RenameMethodsBase(type):\n    \"\"\"\n    Handles the deprecation paths when renaming a method.\n\n    It does the following:\n        1) Define the new method if missing and complain about it.\n        2) Define the old method if missing.\n        3) Complain whenever an old method is called.\n\n    See #15363 for more details.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    renamed_methods = ()\n\n    def __new__(cls, name, bases, attrs):\n        new_class = super().__new__(cls, name, bases, attrs)\n\n        for base in inspect.getmro(new_class):\n            class_name = base.__name__\n            for renamed_method in cls.renamed_methods:\n                old_method_name = renamed_method[0]\n                old_method = base.__dict__.get(old_method_name)\n                new_method_name = renamed_method[1]\n                new_method = base.__dict__.get(new_method_name)\n                deprecation_warning = renamed_method[2]\n                wrapper = warn_about_renamed_method(class_name, *renamed_method)\n\n                # Define the new method if missing and complain about it\n                if not new_method and old_method:\n                    warnings.warn(\n                        \"`%s.%s` method should be renamed `%s`.\"\n                        % (class_name, old_method_name, new_method_name),\n                        deprecation_warning,\n                        2,\n                    )\n                    setattr(base, new_method_name, old_method)\n                    setattr(base, old_method_name, wrapper(old_method))\n\n                # Define the old method as a wrapped call to the new method.\n                if not old_method and new_method:\n                    setattr(base, old_method_name, wrapper(new_method))\n\n        return new_class\n\n\ndef deprecate_posargs(deprecation_warning, remappable_names, /):\n    \"\"\"\n    Function/method decorator to deprecate some or all positional arguments.\n\n    The decorated function will map any positional arguments after the ``*`` to\n    the corresponding keyword arguments and issue a deprecation warning.\n\n    The decorator takes two arguments: a RemovedInDjangoXXWarning warning\n    category and a list of parameter names that have been changed from\n    positional-or-keyword to keyword-only, in their original positional order.\n\n    Works on both functions and methods. To apply to a class constructor,\n    decorate its __init__() method. To apply to a staticmethod or classmethod,\n    use @deprecate_posargs after @staticmethod or @classmethod.\n\n    Example: to deprecate passing option1 or option2 as posargs, change::\n\n        def some_func(request, option1, option2=True):\n            ...\n\n    to::\n\n        @deprecate_posargs(RemovedInDjangoXXWarning, [\"option1\", \"option2\"])\n        def some_func(request, *, option1, option2=True):\n            ...\n\n    After the deprecation period, remove the decorator (but keep the ``*``)::\n\n        def some_func(request, *, option1, option2=True):\n            ...\n\n    Caution: during the deprecation period, do not add any new *positional*\n    parameters or change the remaining ones. For example, this attempt to add a\n    new param would break code using the deprecated posargs::\n\n        @deprecate_posargs(RemovedInDjangoXXWarning, [\"option1\", \"option2\"])\n        def some_func(request, wrong_new_param=None, *, option1, option2=True):\n            # Broken: existing code may pass a value intended as option1 in the\n            # wrong_new_param position.\n            ...\n\n    However, it's acceptable to add new *keyword-only* parameters and to\n    re-order the existing ones, so long as the list passed to\n    @deprecate_posargs is kept in the original posargs order. This change will\n    work without breaking existing code::\n\n        @deprecate_posargs(RemovedInDjangoXXWarning, [\"option1\", \"option2\"])\n        def some_func(request, *, new_param=None, option2=True, option1):\n            ...\n\n    The @deprecate_posargs decorator adds a small amount of overhead. In most\n    cases it won't be significant, but use with care in performance-critical\n    code paths.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(func):\n        if isinstance(func, type):\n            raise TypeError(\n                \"@deprecate_posargs cannot be applied to a class. (Apply it \"\n                \"to the __init__ method.)\"\n            )\n        if isinstance(func, classmethod):\n            raise TypeError(\"Apply @classmethod before @deprecate_posargs.\")\n        if isinstance(func, staticmethod):\n            raise TypeError(\"Apply @staticmethod before @deprecate_posargs.\")\n\n        params = inspect.signature(func).parameters\n        num_by_kind = Counter(param.kind for param in params.values())\n\n        if num_by_kind[inspect.Parameter.VAR_POSITIONAL] > 0:\n            raise TypeError(\n                \"@deprecate_posargs() cannot be used with variable positional `*args`.\"\n            )\n\n        num_positional_params = (\n            num_by_kind[inspect.Parameter.POSITIONAL_ONLY]\n            + num_by_kind[inspect.Parameter.POSITIONAL_OR_KEYWORD]\n        )\n        num_keyword_only_params = num_by_kind[inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY]\n        if num_keyword_only_params < 1:\n            raise TypeError(\n                \"@deprecate_posargs() requires at least one keyword-only parameter \"\n                \"(after a `*` entry in the parameters list).\"\n            )\n        if any(\n            name not in params or params[name].kind != inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY\n            for name in remappable_names\n        ):\n            raise TypeError(\n                \"@deprecate_posargs() requires all remappable_names to be \"\n                \"keyword-only parameters.\"\n            )\n\n        num_remappable_args = len(remappable_names)\n        max_positional_args = num_positional_params + num_remappable_args\n\n        func_name = func.__name__\n        if func_name == \"__init__\":\n            # In the warning, show \"ClassName()\" instead of \"__init__()\".\n            # The class isn't defined yet, but its name is in __qualname__.\n            # Some examples of __qualname__:\n            # - ClassName.__init__\n            # - Nested.ClassName.__init__\n            # - MyTests.test_case.<locals>.ClassName.__init__\n            local_name = func.__qualname__.rsplit(\"<locals>.\", 1)[-1]\n            class_name = local_name.replace(\".__init__\", \"\")\n            func_name = class_name\n\n        def remap_deprecated_args(args, kwargs):\n            \"\"\"\n            Move deprecated positional args to kwargs and issue a warning.\n            Return updated (args, kwargs).\n            \"\"\"\n            if (num_positional_args := len(args)) > max_positional_args:\n                raise TypeError(\n                    f\"{func_name}() takes at most {max_positional_args} positional \"\n                    f\"argument(s) (including {num_remappable_args} deprecated) but \"\n                    f\"{num_positional_args} were given.\"\n                )\n\n            # Identify which of the _potentially remappable_ params are\n            # actually _being remapped_ in this particular call.\n            remapped_names = remappable_names[\n                : num_positional_args - num_positional_params\n            ]\n            conflicts = set(remapped_names) & set(kwargs)\n            if conflicts:\n                # Report duplicate names in the original parameter order.\n                conflicts_str = \", \".join(\n                    f\"'{name}'\" for name in remapped_names if name in conflicts\n                )\n                raise TypeError(\n                    f\"{func_name}() got both deprecated positional and keyword \"\n                    f\"argument values for {conflicts_str}.\"\n                )\n\n            # Do the remapping.\n            remapped_kwargs = dict(\n                zip(remapped_names, args[num_positional_params:], strict=True)\n            )\n            remaining_args = args[:num_positional_params]\n            updated_kwargs = kwargs | remapped_kwargs\n\n            # Issue the deprecation warning.\n            remapped_names_str = \", \".join(f\"'{name}'\" for name in remapped_names)\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Passing positional argument(s) {remapped_names_str} to {func_name}() \"\n                \"is deprecated. Use keyword arguments instead.\",\n                deprecation_warning,\n                skip_file_prefixes=django_file_prefixes(),\n            )\n\n            return remaining_args, updated_kwargs\n\n        if iscoroutinefunction(func):\n\n            @functools.wraps(func)\n            async def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n                if len(args) > num_positional_params:\n                    args, kwargs = remap_deprecated_args(args, kwargs)\n                return await func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        else:\n\n            @functools.wraps(func)\n            def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n                if len(args) > num_positional_params:\n                    args, kwargs = remap_deprecated_args(args, kwargs)\n                return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        return wrapper\n\n    return decorator\n\n\nclass MiddlewareMixin:\n    sync_capable = True\n    async_capable = True\n\n    def __init__(self, get_response):\n        if get_response is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"get_response must be provided.\")\n        self.get_response = get_response\n        # If get_response is a coroutine function, turns us into async mode so\n        # a thread is not consumed during a whole request.\n        self.async_mode = iscoroutinefunction(self.get_response)\n        if self.async_mode:\n            # Mark the class as async-capable, but do the actual switch inside\n            # __call__ to avoid swapping out dunder methods.\n            markcoroutinefunction(self)\n        super().__init__()\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<%s get_response=%s>\" % (\n            self.__class__.__qualname__,\n            getattr(\n                self.get_response,\n                \"__qualname__\",\n                self.get_response.__class__.__name__,\n            ),\n        )\n\n    def __call__(self, request):\n        # Exit out to async mode, if needed\n        if self.async_mode:\n            return self.__acall__(request)\n        response = None\n        if hasattr(self, \"process_request\"):\n            response = self.process_request(request)\n        response = response or self.get_response(request)\n        if hasattr(self, \"process_response\"):\n            response = self.process_response(request, response)\n        return response\n\n    async def __acall__(self, request):\n        \"\"\"\n        Async version of __call__ that is swapped in when an async request\n        is running.\n        \"\"\"\n        response = None\n        if hasattr(self, \"process_request\"):\n            response = await sync_to_async(\n                self.process_request,\n                thread_sensitive=True,\n            )(request)\n        response = response or await self.get_response(request)\n        if hasattr(self, \"process_response\"):\n            response = await sync_to_async(\n                self.process_response,\n                thread_sensitive=True,\n            )(request, response)\n        return response\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

        " import os",

  "description": "Fix markcoroutinefunction imports in all files"
}

Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTest to find the bug in CONN_MAX_AGE = None handling.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport time\n\n# Test the actual condition\nprint(\"=== Testing the condition logic ===\\n\")\n\n# Case 1: CONN_MAX_AGE = None\nprint(\"Case 1: CONN_MAX_AGE = None\")\nclose_at = None\nmax_age = None\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: NO\")\n\n# Case 2: CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\nprint(\"\\nCase 2: CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\")\nclose_at = time.monotonic() + 0  # This is what the code does\nmax_age = 0\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)  # Small delay\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time: {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: NO\")\n\n# Case 3: CONN_MAX_AGE = 10\nprint(\"\\nCase 3: CONN_MAX_AGE = 10\")\nstart_time = time.monotonic()\nclose_at = start_time + 10\nmax_age = 10\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (immediately after): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (immediately): NO\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (immediately): NO\")\n\n# Wait and check again\ntime.sleep(0.5)\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (after 0.5s): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after 0.5s): NO\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after 0.5s): NO\")\n\n# Wait for the timeout\ntime.sleep(9.6)  # Total ~10.1 seconds\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (after ~10s): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after ~10s): YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after ~10s): NO\")\n\n# ===== Now let's think about what MIGHT be broken =====\nprint(\"\\n=== Investigating potential bugs ===\\n\")\n\nprint(\"What if the condition should be inverted?\")\nprint(\"If the condition was: if close_at is NOT None OR time.monotonic() >= close_at:\")\nprint(\"  This would close connections for CONN_MAX_AGE = None (BUG!) and all others\")\nprint(\"  This could explain the reported issue!\")\n\nprint(\"\\nWhat if close_at logic was inverted?\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = None: close_at = time.monotonic() (not None)\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = 0: close_at = None\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = N: close_at = time.monotonic() + N\")\nprint(\"  This would be BACKWARDS from the current code!\")\nprint(\"  This would cause persistent connections to close immediately!\")\n\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_connection_logic.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTest to find the bug in CONN_MAX_AGE = None handling.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport time\n\n# Test the actual condition\nprint(\"=== Testing the condition logic ===\\n\")\n\n# Case 1: CONN_MAX_AGE = None\nprint(\"Case 1: CONN_MAX_AGE = None\")\nclose_at = None\nmax_age = None\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: NO\")\n\n# Case 2: CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\nprint(\"\\nCase 2: CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\")\nclose_at = time.monotonic() + 0  # This is what the code does\nmax_age = 0\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)  # Small delay\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time: {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close: NO\")\n\n# Case 3: CONN_MAX_AGE = 10\nprint(\"\\nCase 3: CONN_MAX_AGE = 10\")\nstart_time = time.monotonic()\nclose_at = start_time + 10\nmax_age = 10\nprint(f\"  max_age: {max_age}\")\nprint(f\"  close_at: {close_at}\")\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (immediately after): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\n# Current condition in the code:\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (immediately): NO\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (immediately): NO\")\n\n# Wait and check again\ntime.sleep(0.5)\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (after 0.5s): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after 0.5s): NO\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after 0.5s): NO\")\n\n# Wait for the timeout\ntime.sleep(9.6)  # Total ~10.1 seconds\ncurrent_time = time.monotonic()\nprint(f\"  current_time (after ~10s): {current_time}\")\nprint(f\"  current_time >= close_at: {current_time >= close_at}\")\nif close_at is not None and current_time >= close_at:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after ~10s): YES\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"  -> Should close (after ~10s): NO\")\n\n# ===== Now let's think about what MIGHT be broken =====\nprint(\"\\n=== Investigating potential bugs ===\\n\")\n\nprint(\"What if the condition should be inverted?\")\nprint(\"If the condition was: if close_at is NOT None OR time.monotonic() >= close_at:\")\nprint(\"  This would close connections for CONN_MAX_AGE = None (BUG!) and all others\")\nprint(\"  This could explain the reported issue!\")\n\nprint(\"\\nWhat if close_at logic was inverted?\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = None: close_at = time.monotonic() (not None)\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = 0: close_at = None\")\nprint(\"If CONN_MAX_AGE = N: close_at = time.monotonic() + N\")\nprint(\"  This would be BACKWARDS from the current code!\")\nprint(\"  This would cause persistent connections to close immediately!\")\n\n",

  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "=== Testing the condition logic ===\n\nCase 1: CONN_MAX_AGE = None\n  max_age: None\n  close_at: None\n  -> Should close: NO\n\nCase 2: CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\n  max_age: 0\n  close_at: 275240.817139442\n  current_time: 275240.827227261\n  current_time >= close_at: True\n  -> Should close: YES\n\nCase 3: CONN_MAX_AGE = 10\n  max_age: 10\n  close_at: 275250.82724079\n  current_time (immediately after): 275240.827244099\n  current_time >= close_at: False\n  -> Should close (immediately): NO\n  current_time (after 0.5s): 275241.327329765\n  current_time >= close_at: False\n  -> Should close (after 0.5s): NO\n  current_time (after ~10s): 275250.927438412\n  current_time >= close_at: True\n  -> Should close (after ~10s): YES\n\n=== Investigating potential bugs ===\n\nWhat if the condition should be inverted?\nIf the condition was: if close_at is NOT None OR time.monotonic() >= close_at:\n  This would close connections for CONN_MAX_AGE = None (BUG!) and all others\n  This could explain the reported issue!\n\nWhat if close_at logic was inverted?\nIf CONN_MAX_AGE = None: close_at = time.monotonic() (not None)\nIf CONN_MAX_AGE = 0: close_at = None\nIf CONN_MAX_AGE = N: close_at = time.monotonic() + N\n  This would be BACKWARDS from the current code!\n  This would cause persistent connections to close immediately!"
}

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
  "limit": 30,
  "offset": 591

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n        or if it outlived its maximum age.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.connection is not None:\n            self.health_check_done = False\n            # If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\n            # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\n            if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n            # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n            if self.errors_occurred:\n                if self.is_usable():\n                    self.errors_occurred = False\n                    self.health_check_done = True\n                else:\n                    self.close()\n                    return\n\n            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n\n    @property",
    "filePath": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 591,
    "totalLines": 790
  },
  "type": "text"
}
62. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git log -p -S \"self.close_at = None if max_age is None\" -- django/db/backends/base/base.py | head -100",
  "description": "Search for changes to close_at assignment logic"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 28308078f397d1de36fd0da417ac7da2544ba12d\nAuthor: Tim Graham <timograham@gmail.com>\nDate:   Mon Jan 12 15:20:40 2015 -0500\n\n    Fixed #22603 -- Reorganized classes in django.db.backends.\n\ndiff --git a/django/db/backends/base/base.py b/django/db/backends/base/base.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000..fb9260d8ca\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/django/db/backends/base/base.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,507 @@\n+from collections import deque\n+from contextlib import contextmanager\n+import time\n+import warnings\n+\n+from django.conf import settings\n+from django.db import DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS\n+from django.db.backends.signals import connection_created\n+from django.db.backends import utils\n+from django.db.transaction import TransactionManagementError\n+from django.db.utils import DatabaseError, DatabaseErrorWrapper\n+from django.utils.functional import cached_property\n+try:\n+    from django.utils.six.moves import _thread as thread\n+except ImportError:\n+    from django.utils.six.moves import _dummy_thread as thread\n+\n+\n+NO_DB_ALIAS = '__no_db__'\n+\n+\n+class BaseDatabaseWrapper(object):\n+    \"\"\"\n+    Represents a database connection.\n+    \"\"\"\n+    # Mapping of Field objects to their column types.\n+    data_types = {}\n+    # Mapping of Field objects to their SQL suffix such as AUTOINCREMENT.\n+    data_types_suffix = {}\n+    # Mapping of Field objects to their SQL for CHECK constraints.\n+    data_type_check_constraints = {}\n+    ops = None\n+    vendor = 'unknown'\n+    SchemaEditorClass = None\n+\n+    queries_limit = 9000\n+\n+    def __init__(self, settings_dict, alias=DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS,\n+                 allow_thread_sharing=False):\n+        # Connection related attributes.\n+        # The underlying database connection.\n+        self.connection = None\n+        # `settings_dict` should be a dictionary containing keys such as\n+        # NAME, USER, etc. It's called `settings_dict` instead of `settings`\n+        # to disambiguate it from Django settings modules.\n+        self.settings_dict = settings_dict\n+        self.alias = alias\n+        # Query logging in debug mode or when explicitly enabled.\n+        self.queries_log = deque(maxlen=self.queries_limit)\n+        self.force_debug_cursor = False\n+\n+        # Transaction related attributes.\n+        # Tracks if the connection is in autocommit mode. Per PEP 249, by\n+        # default, it isn't.\n+        self.autocommit = False\n+        # Tracks if the connection is in a transaction managed by 'atomic'.\n+        self.in_atomic_block = False\n+        # Increment to generate unique savepoint ids.\n+        self.savepoint_state = 0\n+        # List of savepoints created by 'atomic'.\n+        self.savepoint_ids = []\n+        # Tracks if the outermost 'atomic' block should commit on exit,\n+        # ie. if autocommit was active on entry.\n+        self.commit_on_exit = True\n+        # Tracks if the transaction should be rolled back to the next\n+        # available savepoint because of an exception in an inner block.\n+        self.needs_rollback = False\n+\n+        # Connection termination related attributes.\n+        self.close_at = None\n+        self.closed_in_transaction = False\n+        self.errors_occurred = False\n+\n+        # Thread-safety related attributes.\n+        self.allow_thread_sharing = allow_thread_sharing\n+        self._thread_ident = thread.get_ident()\n+\n+    @property\n+    def queries_logged(self):\n+        return self.force_debug_cursor or settings.DEBUG\n+\n+    @property\n+    def queries(self):\n+        if len(self.queries_log) == self.queries_log.maxlen:\n+            warnings.warn(\n+                \"Limit for query logging exceeded, only the last {} queries \"\n+                \"will be returned.\".format(self.queries_log.maxlen))\n+        return list(self.queries_log)"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/backends/base/test_base.py",
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 352
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n        old_connection = connection.connection\n        # Simulate request_finished.\n        connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n        # Persistent connections are enabled (connection is not).\n        self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n        # Health checks are not performed.\n        with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n            self.run_query()\n            # Health check wasn't performed and the connection is unchanged.\n            self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n            self.run_query()\n            # The connection is unchanged after the next query either during\n            # the current \"request\".\n            self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n\n    @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n    def test_set_autocommit_health_checks_enabled(self):\n        self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n        self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)",
    "filePath": "/workspace/tests/backends/base/test_base.py",
    "numLines": 20,
    "startLine": 352,
    "totalLines": 439
  },
  "type": "text"
}
64. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 344
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####",
    "filePath": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
    "numLines": 20,
    "startLine": 344,
    "totalLines": 790
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git log --all --grep=\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\|connection.*close\\|persistent.*connection\" --oneline | head -20",
  "description": "Search git history for connection-related commits"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "3201a895cb Added DatabaseCreation.destroy_test_db_connection_close_method hook.\nab4bb5b2f9 [5.1.x] Fixed #33497 -- Doc'd that persistent DB connections should be disabled in ASGI and async modes.\nc779808c8e [5.2.x] Fixed #33497 -- Doc'd that persistent DB connections should be disabled in ASGI and async modes.\n8713e4ae96 Fixed #33497 -- Doc'd that persistent DB connections should be disabled in ASGI and async modes.\n4ce59f602e Fixed #30398 -- Added CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS database setting.\na6cc8b80cb [3.0.x] Fixed #31400 -- Doc'd the expected type of CONN_MAX_AGE database option.\nbec4dea844 Fixed #31400 -- Doc'd the expected type of CONN_MAX_AGE database option.\na9c6ab0356 Fixed #30619 -- Made runserver --nothreading use single threaded WSGIServer.\ne1721ece48 [2.1.x] Fixed #29849 -- Fixed keep-alive support in runserver.\n934acf1126 Fixed keep-alive support in manage.py runserver.\nb0f4eecfa7 Ignored repeated calls to connection.close().\n9bbb43dd1a [1.7.x] Ignored repeated calls to connection.close().\n6fa7d7c594 [1.6.x] Fixed #21553 -- Ensured unusable database connections get closed.\nf6f188ffc7 [1.7.x] Fixed #21553 -- Ensured unusable database connections get closed.\n5f2f47fdfc Fixed #21553 -- Ensured unusable database connections get closed.\n3d595c3bc3 Fixed #20215 -- Disabled persistent connections by default.\n93af822c00 Improved recovery when the connection is closed in an atomic block.\n2ee21d9f0d Implemented persistent database connections.\n85712a5355 Made sure connections are actually closed in backends tests\n7c657b2416 Fixed #15802 -- pyscopg2 sometimes fail to close the connection when it's already closed by the server, Thanks Rick van Hattem"
}
67. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTest to reproduce the CONN_MAX_AGE = None bug.\nThis simulates what the Django code does.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': None,  # Persistent connections\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\n\nprint(\"Testing CONN_MAX_AGE = None behavior\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nassert connection.close_at is None, f\"close_at should be None, got {connection.close_at}\"\nprint(\"   \u2713 close_at is None (correct for persistent connections)\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\nprint(f\"\\n2. Old connection object id: {id(old_conn)}\")\n\nprint(\"\\n3. Calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\n\nif connection.connection is None:\n    print(\"   \u2717 FAILED: Connection was closed when it shouldn't be!\")\n    print(f\"   Old connection id: {id(old_conn)}\")\n    print(f\"   Current connection: {connection.connection}\")\n    sys.exit(1)\nelif connection.connection is old_conn:\n    print(f\"   \u2713 PASSED: Connection is still the same object\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"   \u2717 FAILED: Connection was replaced with a different object!\")\n    print(f\"   Old connection id: {id(old_conn)}\")\n    print(f\"   New connection id: {id(connection.connection)}\")\n    sys.exit(1)\n\nprint(\"\\n\u2713 All tests passed!\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_persistent_conn.py"
}
Output

  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTest to reproduce the CONN_MAX_AGE = None bug.\nThis simulates what the Django code does.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': None,  # Persistent connections\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\n\nprint(\"Testing CONN_MAX_AGE = None behavior\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nassert connection.close_at is None, f\"close_at should be None, got {connection.close_at}\"\nprint(\"   \u2713 close_at is None (correct for persistent connections)\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\nprint(f\"\\n2. Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTest to verify CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 behavior.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\n\nprint(\"Testing CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 behavior\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nassert connection.close_at is not None, f\"close_at should not be None for CONN_MAX_AGE=0\"\nprint(f\"   \u2713 close_at is set ({connection.close_at})\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\nprint(f\"\\n2. Old connection object id: {id(old_conn)}\")\n\nimport time\nprint(f\"\\n3. Waiting a tiny bit and calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)  # Give a tiny bit of time\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\n\nif connection.connection is None:\n    print(\"   \u2713 PASSED: Connection was closed as expected for CONN_MAX_AGE=0\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"   \u2717 FAILED: Connection was NOT closed!\")\n    print(f\"   Old connection id: {id(old_conn)}\")\n    print(f\"   Current connection id: {id(connection.connection)}\")\n    sys.exit(1)\n\nprint(\"\\n\u2713 All tests passed!\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_conn_max_age_0.py"
}

Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nDebug test to understand why CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 is not working.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Debugging CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic(): {time.monotonic()}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at - now: {connection.close_at - time.monotonic() if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Waiting a bit...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic(): {time.monotonic()}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at - now: {connection.close_at - time.monotonic() if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic() >= close_at: {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n3. Manually checking the condition...\")\nprint(f\"   close_at is not None: {connection.close_at is not None}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic() >= close_at: {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= close_at: {connection.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n4. Checking close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() implementation...\")\nif connection.connection is not None:\n    print(f\"   connection is not None: True\")\n    print(f\"   checking autocommit...\")\n    print(f\"   get_autocommit(): {connection.get_autocommit()}\")\n    print(f\"   settings autocommit: {connection.settings_dict['AUTOCOMMIT']}\")\n    if connection.get_autocommit() != connection.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n        print(f\"   -> Would close due to autocommit mismatch\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> No autocommit mismatch\")\n    print(f\"   errors_occurred: {connection.errors_occurred}\")\n    if connection.errors_occurred:\n        print(f\"   -> Would check if usable\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> No errors occurred\")\n    print(f\"   Checking close_at condition...\")\n    if connection.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at:\n        print(f\"   -> Would close due to age\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> Would NOT close due to age\")\n        print(f\"      close_at={connection.close_at}, time.monotonic()={time.monotonic()}\")\n        print(f\"      Condition: {connection.close_at is not None} and {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. Now calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection after: {connection.connection}\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_debug_conn_max_age_0.py"
}

  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nDebug test to understand why CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 is not working.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Debugging CONN_MAX_AGE = 0\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {connection.connection}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic(): {time.monotonic()}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at - now: {connection.close_at - time.monotonic() if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Waiting a bit...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic(): {time.monotonic()}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.close_at: {connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at - now: {connection.close_at - time.monotonic() if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic() >= close_at: {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at if connection.close_at else 'N/A'}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n3. Manually checking the condition...\")\nprint(f\"   close_at is not None: {connection.close_at is not None}\")\nprint(f\"   time.monotonic() >= close_at: {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\nprint(f\"   close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= close_at: {connection.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n4. Checking close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() implementation...\")\nif connection.connection is not None:\n    print(f\"   connection is not None: True\")\n    print(f\"   checking autocommit...\")\n    print(f\"   get_autocommit(): {connection.get_autocommit()}\")\n    print(f\"   settings autocommit: {connection.settings_dict['AUTOCOMMIT']}\")\n    if connection.get_autocommit() != connection.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n        print(f\"   -> Would close due to autocommit mismatch\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> No autocommit mismatch\")\n    print(f\"   errors_occurred: {connection.errors_occurred}\")\n    if connection.errors_occurred:\n        print(f\"   -> Would check if usable\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> No errors occurred\")\n    print(f\"   Checking close_at condition...\")\n    if connection.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at:\n        print(f\"   -> Would close due to age\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"   -> Would NOT close due to age\")\n        print(f\"      close_at={connection.close_at}, time.monotonic()={time.monotonic()}\")\n        print(f\"      Condition: {connection.close_at is not None} and {time.monotonic() >= connection.close_at}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. Now calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection after: {connection.connection}\")\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/test_debug_conn_max_age_0.py",

73. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nDebug test to understand why CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 is not working.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Debugging CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 v2\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Waiting a bit...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)\n\nprint(f\"\\n3. Before calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {id(connection.connection)}\")\n\n# Let's manually trace through close_if_unusable_or_obsolete\nprint(f\"\\n4. Manually tracing close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\n\n# Add some debugging to the close method\noriginal_close = connection.close\n\ndef debug_close():\n    print(f\"   -> close() called\")\n    print(f\"      in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\n    print(f\"      closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\n    print(f\"      connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\n    return original_close()\n\nconnection.close = debug_close\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. Now calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection after: {connection.connection}\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_debug_conn_max_age_0_v2.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nDebug test to understand why CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 is not working.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Debugging CONN_MAX_AGE = 0 v2\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\n\nold_conn = connection.connection\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Waiting a bit...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)\n\nprint(f\"\\n3. Before calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.connection: {id(connection.connection)}\")\n\n# Let's manually trace through close_if_unusable_or_obsolete\nprint(f\"\\n4. Manually tracing close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\n\n# Add some debugging to the close method\noriginal_close = connection.close\n\ndef debug_close():\n    print(f\"   -> close() called\")\n    print(f\"      in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\n    print(f\"      closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\n    print(f\"      connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\n    return original_close()\n\nconnection.close = debug_close\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. Now calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\nprint(f\"   connection.connection after: {connection.connection}\")\n",

  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nDebug the close() method to see why it's not setting connection = None.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Debugging close() method\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nold_conn_id = id(connection.connection)\nprint(f\"   connection id: {old_conn_id}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Before close()...\")\nprint(f\"   connection.closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nprint(f\"   connection.connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\n\n# Manually call close\nprint(f\"\\n3. Manually calling close()...\")\n\n# Trace through the close() method logic\nprint(f\"   Checking validate_thread_sharing()...\")\n# Skip this for now\n\nprint(f\"   Checking closed_in_transaction or connection is None...\")\nif connection.closed_in_transaction or connection.connection is None:\n    print(f\"      Would return early! closed_in_transaction={connection.closed_in_transaction}, connection is None={connection.connection is None}\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"      Proceeding with close...\")\n\ntry:\n    print(f\"   Calling _close()...\")\n    connection._close()\n    print(f\"   _close() succeeded\")\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"   _close() failed: {e}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n4. After _close() but checking if atomic block...\")\nprint(f\"   connection.in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\nif connection.in_atomic_block:\n    print(f\"   -> In atomic block, setting closed_in_transaction and needs_rollback\")\n    connection.closed_in_transaction = True\n    connection.needs_rollback = True\nelse:\n    print(f\"   -> NOT in atomic block, setting connection = None\")\n    connection.connection = None\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. After manual close simulation...\")\nprint(f\"   connection.connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\nif connection.connection is not None:\n    print(f\"   connection id: {id(connection.connection)}\")\n",

77. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 344
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n\n    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 344,
    "totalLines": 790
  },
  "type": "text"
}
78. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nTrace through the close() method step by step.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,  # Close immediately\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\nimport time\n\nprint(\"Tracing close() method\\n\")\n\n# Connect to the database\nprint(\"1. Connecting to database...\")\nconnection.connect()\nold_conn_obj = connection.connection\nprint(f\"   connection object: {old_conn_obj}\")\nprint(f\"   connection id: {id(old_conn_obj)}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n2. Patching close() to add tracing...\")\noriginal_close = connection.close\n\ndef traced_close():\n    print(f\"   >> close() called\")\n    print(f\"      closed_in_transaction: {connection.closed_in_transaction}\")\n    print(f\"      connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\n    if connection.closed_in_transaction or connection.connection is None:\n        print(f\"      -> Returning early\")\n        return\n\n    try:\n        print(f\"      -> Calling _close()\")\n        connection._close()\n        print(f\"      -> _close() completed\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"      -> _close() raised: {e}\")\n        raise\n    finally:\n        print(f\"      -> In finally block\")\n        print(f\"         in_atomic_block: {connection.in_atomic_block}\")\n        if connection.in_atomic_block:\n            print(f\"         -> Setting closed_in_transaction and needs_rollback\")\n            connection.closed_in_transaction = True\n            connection.needs_rollback = True\n        else:\n            print(f\"         -> Setting connection = None\")\n            connection.connection = None\n        print(f\"      -> After finally: connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\n\nconnection.close = traced_close\n\nprint(f\"\\n3. Waiting a bit...\")\ntime.sleep(0.01)\n\nprint(f\"\\n4. Calling close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nconnection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n\nprint(f\"\\n5. After close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()...\")\nprint(f\"   connection is None: {connection.connection is None}\")\nprint(f\"   connection object: {connection.connection}\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_trace_close.py"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n\n    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n",

    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 344,

{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\"\"\"\nCheck what type of object the connection is.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\nimport os\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\nimport django\nfrom django.conf import settings\n\n# Configure Django settings\nif not settings.configured:\n    settings.configure(\n        DEBUG=True,\n        DATABASES={\n            'default': {\n                'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n                'NAME': ':memory:',\n                'CONN_MAX_AGE': 0,\n                'CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS': False,\n                'AUTOCOMMIT': True,\n            }\n        },\n        INSTALLED_APPS=[\n            'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n            'django.contrib.auth',\n        ],\n    )\n    django.setup()\n\nfrom django.db import connection\n\nprint(f\"Type of connection: {type(connection)}\")\nprint(f\"connection.__class__: {connection.__class__}\")\nprint(f\"connection.__class__.__module__: {connection.__class__.__module__}\")\nprint(f\"connection.__class__.__name__: {connection.__class__.__name__}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nMRO:\")\nfor cls in connection.__class__.__mro__:\n    print(f\"  {cls}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nIs it a ConnectionProxy? {hasattr(connection, '__wrapped__')}\")\nprint(f\"Dir: {[x for x in dir(connection) if not x.startswith('_')][:10]}\")\n\n# Check if close is a method or a function\nprint(f\"\\nType of connection.close: {type(connection.close)}\")\nprint(f\"connection.close: {connection.close}\")\n\n# Try to get the actual DatabaseWrapper\nfrom django.db import connections\ndw = connections['default']\nprint(f\"\\nType of dw (DatabaseWrapper): {type(dw)}\")\nprint(f\"dw.__class__.__name__: {dw.__class__.__name__}\")\nprint(f\"Type of dw.close: {type(dw.close)}\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_check_connection_type.py"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "from asgiref.local import Local\n\nfrom django.conf import settings as django_settings\nfrom django.utils.functional import cached_property\n\n\nclass ConnectionProxy:\n    \"\"\"Proxy for accessing a connection object's attributes.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, connections, alias):\n        self.__dict__[\"_connections\"] = connections\n        self.__dict__[\"_alias\"] = alias\n\n    def __getattr__(self, item):\n        return getattr(self._connections[self._alias], item)\n\n    def __setattr__(self, name, value):\n        return setattr(self._connections[self._alias], name, value)\n\n    def __delattr__(self, name):\n        return delattr(self._connections[self._alias], name)\n\n    def __contains__(self, key):\n        return key in self._connections[self._alias]\n\n    def __eq__(self, other):\n        return self._connections[self._alias] == other\n\n\nclass ConnectionDoesNotExist(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\nclass BaseConnectionHandler:\n    settings_name = None\n    exception_class = ConnectionDoesNotExist\n    thread_critical = False\n\n    def __init__(self, settings=None):\n        self._settings = settings\n        self._connections = Local(self.thread_critical)\n\n    @cached_property\n    def settings(self):\n        self._settings = self.configure_settings(self._settings)\n        return self._settings\n\n    def configure_settings(self, settings):\n        if settings is None:\n            settings = getattr(django_settings, self.settings_name)\n        return settings\n\n    def create_connection(self, alias):\n        raise NotImplementedError(\"Subclasses must implement create_connection().\")\n\n    def __getitem__(self, alias):\n        try:\n            return getattr(self._connections, alias)\n        except AttributeError:\n            if alias not in self.settings:\n                raise self.exception_class(f\"The connection '{alias}' doesn't exist.\")\n        conn = self.create_connection(alias)\n        setattr(self._connections, alias, conn)\n        return conn\n\n    def __setitem__(self, key, value):\n        setattr(self._connections, key, value)\n\n    def __delitem__(self, key):\n        delattr(self._connections, key)\n\n    def __iter__(self):\n        return iter(self.settings)\n\n    def all(self, initialized_only=False):\n        return [\n            self[alias]\n            for alias in self\n            # If initialized_only is True, return only initialized connections.\n            if not initialized_only or hasattr(self._connections, alias)\n        ]\n\n    def close_all(self):\n        for conn in self.all(initialized_only=True):\n            conn.close()\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/django/utils/connection.py",
    "numLines": 86,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 86
  },

The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                import sys\n                print(f\"DEBUG: close() setting connection = None (was {self.connection})\", file=sys.stderr)\n                self.connection = None\n                print(f\"DEBUG: close() after setting: connection = {self.connection}\", file=sys.stderr)",
